# Data Preparation and Feature Engineering {#ch-preparation}

::: epigraph
Trust, but verify.

*Ronald Reagan*
:::

<!-- DEVELOPMENT OUTLINE: preserve this marker until the prose is drafted. -->

## Data Quality and Provenance

## Missing, Noisy, and Inconsistent Data

## Scaling, Normalization, and Transformation

## Encoding Categorical and Structured Variables

## Feature Selection and Dimensionality Reduction

## Leakage and Reproducible Pipelines

## Summary and Exercises

There is no perfect data. In fact, a Kaggle 2017 survey showed that
*dirty data* \"is the most common problem for workers in the data
science realm.\"[@Kaggle:2017] About 50% of survey responses cited it as
a barrier at work.
